title: The Demographic Analysis of Mortality Crises: The Case of Cambodia, 1970-1979
strengths: Major demographic reconstruction; explicit separation between excess mortality and a modeled violent-death residual.
limitations: Violent deaths are not status-separated into civilian versus military/combatant; some components are rough approximations.
notes: Used for the direct-violence envelope and for end-of-period population context, not for a clean civilian/military split.

title: The Boundaries of Genocide: Quantifying the Uncertainty of the Death Toll During the Pol Pot Regime (1975-1979)
strengths: Best compact review of the published violent-death and excess-death literature; explicitly dates the Pol Pot regime interval.
limitations: Still mostly regime-wide and mixed-status; not a direct civilian/combatant ledger.
notes: Used to triangulate a conservative direct-death envelope and to justify keeping excess mortality outside the core DR path.

title: The Demography of Genocide in Southeast Asia: The Death Tolls in Cambodia, 1975-79, and East Timor, 1975-80
strengths: Provides a compact, auditable summary of competing April 1975 population estimates and a correction for the early expulsion of ethnic Vietnamese.
limitations: Not a monthly population series; still a reconstruction from prior work.
notes: Used for the civilian denominator start-point range.

title: The Khmer Rouge National Army: Order of Battle, January 1976
author_or_org: Genocide Studies Program, Yale MacMillan Center
strengths: Direct order-of-battle figure for regular Khmer Rouge forces.
limitations: Single time point; regular forces only.
notes: Used to anchor military person-month interpolation.

title: Cambodia: A Country Study
author_or_org: Federal Research Division, Library of Congress; edited by Russell R. Ross
strengths: Provides a concise force-size estimate at the moment of conquest.
limitations: Retrospective synthesis rather than a primary personnel ledger.
notes: Used as the earliest military denominator anchor.

title: DEMOCRATIC KAMPUCHEA: CAN THE POL POT REGIME SURVIVE?
author_or_org: Bureau of Intelligence and Research, U.S. Department of State
geographic_scope: Eastern Cambodia and national military context
strengths: Time-specific estimate of total Khmer Rouge armed strength and border deployment during the 1978 war with Vietnam.
limitations: Intelligence estimate; phrases as floors ('in excess of', 'at least').
notes: Used as the late-period military denominator anchor and to define the 1978 eastern-border episode.

title: Summary of the Supreme Court Chamber Appeal Judgement, Case 002/01
author_or_org: Extraordinary Chambers in the Courts of Cambodia
geographic_scope: Phnom Penh evacuation, nationwide population transfers, Tuol Po Chrey (Pursat)
date_scope: 1975-04-17 to 1977-12-31 (scope); late April 1975 for Tuol Po Chrey
strengths: High-quality legal finding; conservative minimum for a clearly military victim group.
limitations: Case scope is narrow and does not support national extrapolation for evacuation deaths.
notes: Used as a minimum anchor for military direct deaths and as a reason not to over-extrapolate Population Movement Phase One.

title: Summary of Judgement, Case 002/02
author_or_org: Extraordinary Chambers in the Courts of Cambodia, Trial Chamber
strengths: Strong legal findings on S-21, Cham and Vietnamese targeting, and the civilian-wide nature of the attack.
limitations: Not a national civilian/combatant tally; some victim categories remain mixed-status.
notes: Used to justify a substantial unknown-status bucket rather than forcing status allocation.

title: A History of Democratic Kampuchea (1975-1979)
author_or_org: Khamboly Dy / Documentation Center of Cambodia
strengths: Useful conflict-specific synthesis of regime practices, mass grave mapping, and the status-mixed nature of victims.
limitations: Often qualitative ('thousands', 'hundreds of thousands'); not a statistical ledger.
notes: Used mainly as qualitative constraint on status allocation and for source review.

title: Internal and External Enemy: The Vietnamese Experience During the Khmer Rouge Period
geographic_scope: Cambodia, especially Eastern Zone and Vietnamese minority
strengths: Provides explicit Eastern Zone population context and a focused treatment of the Vietnamese minority.
limitations: Not a complete national ledger; some counts are secondary citations reproduced in the article.
notes: Used for candidate episodeization and for the early ethnic-Vietnamese expulsion correction.
